points.text: COVID-19 can lead to serious complications and death, unlike the common cold or flu, which are generally milder illnesses.
COVID-19 symptoms can include loss of taste or smell, which is not typical for the common cold or flu.
COVID-19 spreads primarily through respiratory droplets, similar to the flu, but can also spread from asymptomatic individuals.
COVID-19 can cause long-term symptoms, known as post-COVID-19 syndrome, which is still being studied.
Older adults and those with certain medical conditions are at higher risk for severe COVID-19 illness compared to the flu.
COVID-19 is caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus, while the common cold and flu are caused by different viruses.
Symptoms of COVID-19 can range from mild to severe, similar to the flu and common cold, making testing necessary for confirmation.
COVID-19 can lead to serious complications, such as difficulty breathing and multisystem inflammatory syndrome in children, unlike typical colds.
COVID-19 symptoms include loss of taste or smell, which are not common in the flu or common cold.
COVID-19 has a longer incubation period of 2 to 14 days, compared to the common cold and flu.
